title: Light Brown Venezuelan Plantain Empanadas
description: Buttery, dense, indulgent Venezuelan dish featuring light brown soft sugar, made with whole30-friendly ingredients and cooked on a wooden board.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
Peel the plantains and slice them into 1-inch rounds.
In a large bowl, mix together the sliced plantains, light brown sugar, and salt.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the eggs and coconut oil.
Add the almond flour to the egg mixture and stir until well combined.
Add the plantain mixture to the egg mixture and stir until a dough forms.
Divide the dough into 8 equal pieces and roll each piece into a ball.
Flatten each ball into a disk shape and place on a wooden board.
Bake the empanadas for 20 minutes, or until lightly browned.
Remove from the oven and let cool for 5 minutes before serving.
